stories.title: Tatum & Brown Each Score 25, Celtics Beat 76ers 108-100 in Game 3

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
TATUM 25. BROWN 25. CELTICS UP 2-1.

Brown dropped 8 STRAIGHT points in the 4th to put Boston ahead for good. Pritchard hit 5 threes off the bench. Maxey had 31 — wasn't enough.

No Embiid, no problem for Boston.

#HoopHeroes #Celtics #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Celtics go to Philly and take Game 3, 108-100.

Maxey scored 31. George had 18. The Sixers fought hard — but Tatum (5/9 from 3) and Brown closed it down the stretch.

Boston leads 2-1. Embiid still out.

#HoopHeroes #Celtics #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

The Boston Celtics showed exactly why they're considered championship contenders — even on the road and without Joel Embiid on the other side, they found a way to get it done.

Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown each finished with 25 points Friday night in Philadelphia as the Celtics won Game 3, 108-100, and took a 2-1 series lead over the Sixers. Tatum was brilliant from beyond the arc, going 5-for-9 on 3-pointers in just his 19th game of the season following Achilles surgery. But it was Brown who delivered the knockout blow — he rattled off 8 consecutive points late in the fourth quarter to push Boston's advantage to 96-92 and take the wind out of Philly's sails.

Payton Pritchard was a revelation off the bench, knocking down five 3-pointers for 15 points. Tyrese Maxey gave Philadelphia everything he had with 31 points, but Paul George (18 pts) was the only other Sixer to provide real offensive support.

Joel Embiid continues to recover from his April 9 appendectomy and is not yet cleared to practice, much less play. The Sixers showed fight — winning Game 2 in Boston — but with their star center out, the margin for error against this Celtics team is razor thin. Game 4 is Sunday in Philadelphia.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Tatum and Brown Each Score 25, Celtics Beat 76ers 108-100 in Game 3

stories.title: LeBron's Tying 3 + Smart's Historic Line — Lakers Beat Rockets 112-108 OT, Lead 3-0

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
DOWN 6 WITH 30 SECONDS LEFT.

LeBron hits a tying 3 with 13 seconds on the clock. Lakers steal OT. Smart scores 8 of 11 OT points. Lakers win 112-108 and lead 3-0.

41 years old. DIFFERENT.

#HoopHeroes #LakeShow #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Marcus Smart: 21 pts, 10 ast, 5 stl, 2 blk in OT win.

That's the first 20/10/5/2 playoff game since MICHAEL JORDAN in 1991.

No KD. Down 6 with 30 seconds left. Lakers win in OT anyway. 3-0.

#HoopHeroes #LakeShow #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

LeBron James refused to let the Los Angeles Lakers lose. With Houston leading by six points and only 30 seconds remaining in regulation, the 41-year-old legend — who had already logged more than 45 minutes — stepped back and buried a tying 3-pointer with 13 seconds on the clock. The Toyota Center fell silent. The Lakers were going to overtime.

And then Marcus Smart took over.

Smart finished with 21 points, 10 assists, 5 steals, and 2 blocks — the first 20/10/5-steal/2-block playoff game since Michael Jordan accomplished it in 1991. He scored 8 of the Lakers' 11 overtime points, including a go-ahead 3-pointer to push the lead to 107-103. Rui Hachimura added 22 points as the Lakers won 112-108 and took a 3-0 series lead.

Kevin Durant was ruled out with a left ankle sprain he suffered in Game 2, leaving Houston without its best player for the third time in four playoff games this series. The Rockets are now on the brink of elimination with no clear timeline on Durant's return.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: LeBron's Tying 3, Smart's Historic Line Lift Lakers Past Rockets in OT — Lakers Lead 3-0

stories.title: Castle 33, Harper 27 — Spurs Stun Blazers 120-108 Without Wembanyama, Lead 2-1

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Down 15 in the 3rd. No Wembanyama. In Portland.

Stephon Castle: 33 pts. Dylan Harper: 27 pts, 10 reb. Spurs 21-5 run. They never looked back.

SAS leads 2-1. Wemby could return Sunday.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Without their franchise player, the Spurs' NEXT generation showed up.

Castle + Harper = 60 pts combined. Spurs win 120-108 in Portland WITHOUT Wembanyama (concussion protocol).

The future is BRIGHT in San Antonio.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Down 15 in the third quarter, on the road in Portland, without their franchise superstar — and the San Antonio Spurs still found a way to win.

Victor Wembanyama sat out Game 3 as he continues through concussion protocol after a terrifying fall in Game 2. With their cornerstone in street clothes on the bench, San Antonio turned to its next generation of stars — and they delivered in stunning fashion.

Stephon Castle, the 2024 No. 4 overall pick, led the charge with 33 points. Dylan Harper, the 2025 No. 1 pick, chipped in 27 points and 10 rebounds. Luke Kornet, starting in Wembanyama's place, added 14 points and 10 rebounds. Together, the Spurs staged a 21-5 run to turn a 15-point deficit into an 88-87 lead entering the fourth quarter, then closed things out 120-108.

Jrue Holiday led Portland with 29 points, but the Blazers could not hold their advantage. The series shifts to San Antonio for Game 4 on Sunday. The Spurs lead 2-1.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Saturday Playoff Slate — Four Series, Four Games

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
SATURDAY NBA PLAYOFF SCHEDULE

1 PM – Pistons-Magic G3 (1-1) NBC
3:30 PM – Thunder-Suns G3 (OKC 2-0) NBC
6 PM – Hawks-Knicks G4 (ATL 2-1) NBC
8:30 PM – Wolves-Nuggets G4 (MIN 2-1) ABC

Four series. Four big games.

#HoopHeroes #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:00 PM ET

Saturday's NBA playoff schedule is loaded from first tip to last buzzer, with four series and four games that could reshape the entire first-round picture.

The action starts at 1 p.m. ET on NBC with the Pistons and Magic playing Game 3 — tied 1-1. Then the Thunder and Suns tip off at 3:30 p.m. ET on NBC, with Oklahoma City holding a 2-0 series lead but doing so without Jalen Williams (left hamstring). At 6 p.m. ET, the Hawks host the Knicks in Game 4 with Atlanta holding a 2-1 series lead after C.J. McCollum's dramatic game-winner in Game 3.

The night closes with one of the biggest storylines in the playoffs: Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves face the Nuggets at 8:30 p.m. ET on ABC, with Minnesota leading 2-1 but Edwards nursing a knee injury from Game 3. Nikola Jokic went just 7-for-26 in Game 3. A 3-1 deficit would put Denver in serious trouble.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Wembanyama Concussion — How Long Is He Out?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama is in concussion protocol and missed Game 3 vs. Portland.

The Spurs won anyway, 120-108. But Game 4 is Sunday — and his return is still uncertain.

Biggest injury storyline in the playoffs right now.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 4:00 PM ET

When Victor Wembanyama tumbled to the floor after contact from Jrue Holiday in Game 2, his jaw hitting the hardwood, the stakes of his absence became immediately clear. He entered the NBA's concussion protocol, missed Game 3 entirely on Friday night, and watched from the bench as the Spurs beat Portland 120-108 anyway.

For one game, San Antonio answered the question nobody wanted to face: Can the Spurs survive without him? Castle and Harper combined for 60 points in a remarkable display. But concussion protocol isn't just a one-game thing. Wembanyama must clear every step of the NBA's return-to-participation program. He completed some cardio and shooting Thursday before the team departed — encouraging, but not definitive.

Game 4 is Sunday afternoon in San Antonio. Wembanyama averaged 25 points, 11.5 rebounds, and 3.1 blocks this season and was the near-unanimous choice for Defensive Player of the Year. He is the most important player on this team, and San Antonio's ceiling in this series depends on his health.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Wembanyama in Concussion Protocol: What His Absence Means for the Spurs' Playoff Run
